D1 Optics L4 Analyze Reference

Temporal Coherence & Coherence Length

narrow the linewidth and watch fringes survive to ever larger path differences

Interference needs the two paths to differ by less than the coherence length. That length is set by the spectral width: the purer the colour, the longer the coherence.

Set the centre wavelength, the linewidth Δλ, and the path difference ΔL. The strip shows fringe contrast; the curve is visibility versus ΔL with the coherence length marked.
